fundamental_rights: Article 19(1)(g) — Right to practise any profession or carry on any occupation, trade or business
Article 21 — Right to Life and Personal Liberty (includes right to livelihood)
Article 14 — Right to Equality before Law
Article 23 — Right against exploitation and forced labour

laws.name: Industrial Disputes Act, 1947
laws.summary: Governs termination, layoffs, and retrenchment. Employers must give notice and pay compensation.
laws.remedy: File complaint with Labour Commissioner or Labour Court

laws.name: Payment of Wages Act, 1936
laws.summary: Ensures timely payment of wages without unauthorised deductions.
laws.remedy: File complaint with Payment of Wages Authority

laws.name: Minimum Wages Act, 1948
laws.summary: Mandates minimum wages for all categories of employees. Violation is a criminal offence.
laws.remedy: Complain to the Inspector under the Act or Labour Commissioner

laws.name: Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952
laws.summary: Mandates employer PF contributions. Non-deposit is a criminal offence.

laws.name: Maternity Benefit Act, 1961 (amended 2017)
laws.summary: Guarantees 26 weeks paid maternity leave. Dismissal during maternity is illegal.
laws.remedy: File complaint with Inspector of Factories or Labour Commissioner

laws.name: POSH Act, 2013 (Sexual Harassment at Workplace)
laws.summary: Every employer must establish an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC). Complaint within 3 months.
laws.remedy: File complaint with ICC or Local Complaints Committee (LCC)

laws.name: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023
laws.summary: Criminal provisions for workplace harassment and assault.
laws.remedy: File FIR at nearest police station

steps: Collect all employment documents: appointment letter, payslips, PF statements, and written communications
Send a written grievance to HR or management via email (keep copies)
If unresolved in 30 days, file a complaint with the District Labour Commissioner
For unpaid wages or PF: approach the Payment of Wages Authority or EPFO Regional Office
For wrongful termination: file a claim with the Labour Court under Industrial Disputes Act
For harassment: file complaint with Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) within 3 months
Consider free legal aid from DLSA (District Legal Services Authority)
